channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Frogtastic Educational Kindergarten and Childcare approval cancelled following regulatory action
item.description: The service approval for Frogtastic Educational Kindergarten and Childcare in Bundaberg has been cancelled following regulatory action under the Education and Care Services National Law.
item.content:encoded: The service approval for Frogtastic Educational Kindergarten and Childcare in Bundaberg has been cancelled following regulatory action under the Education and Care Services National Law.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Firefighters called to ECEC service after child gets stuck in tambourine during play
item.description: A child at Busy Bees Lara required assistance from emergency services after a tambourine became stuck during play, highlighting the importance of active supervision, thoughtful resource selection and effective incident response procedures in early childhood education and care (ECEC) environments.
item.content:encoded: A child at Busy Bees Lara required assistance from emergency services after a tambourine became stuck during play, highlighting the importance of active supervision, thoughtful resource selection and effective incident response procedures in early childhood education and care (ECEC) environments.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Early Childhood Educators invited to have their say in National Sector Survey
item.description: The Fraser Foundation launches Voice of the Educator Survey 2026 to capture the realities, pressures and priorities of Australia’s early learning workforce
item.content:encoded: The Fraser Foundation launches Voice of the Educator Survey 2026 to capture the realities, pressures and priorities of Australia’s early learning workforce
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Trust begins in the early years: what young Australians’ family plans mean for the ECEC sector
item.description: As Australia’s fertility rate reaches record lows, new research suggests decisions about having children are influenced by more than cost-of-living pressures. Trust in the systems that support families, including early childhood education and care, plays an important role in how young Australians view their future.
item.content:encoded: As Australia’s fertility rate reaches record lows, new research suggests decisions about having children are influenced by more than cost-of-living pressures. Trust in the systems that support families, including early childhood education and care, plays an important role in how young Australians view their future.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: More childcare places coming to Scottsdale as Tasmania expands early learning access
item.description: Families in Scottsdale are set to benefit from improved access to early childhood education and care (ECEC), with the Tasmanian Government announcing additional childcare places for the regional community.
item.content:encoded: Families in Scottsdale are set to benefit from improved access to early childhood education and care (ECEC), with the Tasmanian Government announcing additional childcare places for the regional community.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Embark Early Education share price movement highlights investor focus on changing ECEC operating conditions - The Sector
item.description: Embark Early Education share price movement highlights investor focus on changing ECEC operating conditions - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Embark Early Education share price movement highlights investor focus on changing ECEC operating conditions - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Brewarrina voices carry language into classrooms across Australia
item.description: Embark Early Education (ASX:EVO) has attracted investor attention following recent movement in its share price, highlighting the broader market conditions influencing Australia’s listed early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers.
item.content:encoded: Embark Early Education (ASX:EVO) has attracted investor attention following recent movement in its share price, highlighting the broader market conditions influencing Australia’s listed early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: How Z Staffing is building trust through transparency, compliance and child-safe recruitment
item.description: For many approved providers, engaging a recruitment agency is not the first choice, it is often a necessity.
item.content:encoded: For many approved providers, engaging a recruitment agency is not the first choice, it is often a necessity.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: UK regulator takes action against Bright Horizons and the parallels with Australia’s tightening compliance landscape are hard to ignore
item.description: The United Kingdom’s early years regulator has taken regulatory action against Bright Horizons following serious safeguarding concerns and subsequent regulatory activity across the provider’s nursery network.
item.content:encoded: The United Kingdom’s early years regulator has taken regulatory action against Bright Horizons following serious safeguarding concerns and subsequent regulatory activity across the provider’s nursery network.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.content:encoded: ACECQA invites services to register interest in Quality Support Program - The Sector

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 3 July 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, legislation for the National Commissioner comes into effect, continued coverage of child protection reforms in the NT, working with ACCOs necessary for Thriving Kids to be successful and 2026 Children’s Day is just a month away
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, legislation for the National Commissioner comes into effect, continued coverage of child protection reforms in the NT, working with ACCOs necessary for Thriving Kids to be successful and 2026 Children’s Day is just a month away
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 26 June 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, National Commissioner legislation comes into effect, continued coverage of child protection reforms in the NT, coverage of SNAICC's position on Thriving Kids, and continued coverage of Children’s Day 2026 ambassador Marlee Silva.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, National Commissioner legislation comes into effect, continued coverage of child protection reforms in the NT, coverage of SNAICC's position on Thriving Kids, and continued coverage of Children’s Day 2026 ambassador Marlee Silva.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Thriving Kids must be built with Aboriginal communities to succeed
item.description: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.content:encoded: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.content:encoded: Sanctuary Maudsland reaffirms Exceeding Rating under the National Quality Standard - The Sector

item.title: Marlee Silva – National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.dc:creator: News.com.au

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Investing in educators means investing in children
item.description: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.content:encoded: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC
